_meta.notes: Aetna routes through Evicore but has special exceptions: HMO referral requirement, dual submission for outlier plans, and Meritain subsidiary detection. v1.1: Added Meritain detailed workflow, portal URL, detection heuristics, referral NPI rules.

referral.notes: Some Aetna HMO plans require PCP referral. Check via Availity.
referral.npi_rule: Use PCP NPI + Facility NPI ONLY. Do NOT use specialist/reading physician NPI.
referral.scope: Submit referral for ALL codes (HST and in-lab)
referral.critical_note: Even if authorization is not required for HST, the referral may still be required. Check both.

meritain.description: Some plans identified as 'Aetna' are actually Meritain Health (Aetna subsidiary)

detection.id_card: May say 'Meritain' or show Meritain logo
detection.discovery: Sometimes only discovered during benefits call

workflow: CALL for benefit information (no portal lookup for benefits)
During call, ask if authorization is required
If NO auth required → proceed to scheduling
If YES auth required → submit via Meritain portal
meritain.warning: Don't assume Aetna portal/process works for Meritain plans

special_rules.description: Some Aetna outlier plans require auth through BOTH Evicore AND Availity
special_rules.action: If Evicore returns 'No Auth Required', also submit in Availity
special_rules.reason: Certain Aetna plan configurations don't route to Evicore properly. If not required via Availity either, submission will come back as 'not required'.

special_rules.description: Some 'Aetna' plans are actually Meritain Health (Aetna subsidiary)
special_rules.action: Check card/eligibility for Meritain indicators — use meritain workflow

flags.trigger: aetna AND plan_type == hmo AND benefits_show_referral_required
flags.message: Aetna HMO requires PCP referral before any testing. Use PCP NPI + Facility NPI ONLY.
Submit in Availity anyway — some Aetna plans only route through Availity
This is a Meritain plan (Aetna subsidiary). CALL for benefits, auth via meritain.mednecessity.com
